key_insight: Large AI models can turn solid electrolyte discovery from slow, fragmented trial-and-error into a closed-loop, self-improving search process.
engineering_relevance: This framework could accelerate design of safer, higher-performance solid-state batteries by better balancing ion transport, stability, mechanics, and interfacial behavior.
method: Use ML interatomic potentials to connect DFT accuracy with long-timescale ion migration.
Use LLMs to mine literature, generate hypotheses, and support scientific reasoning.
Run closed-loop cycles of AI candidate design, uncertainty-aware selection, simulation, and experiment.
evidence_signal: The abstract frames the approach as a conceptual framework rather than reporting a single benchmark result, emphasizing system-level integration across databases, simulations, and experiments.
watch_item: Practical deployment may be limited by inconsistent data standards, difficult interfacial phenomena, and reproducibility across simulations and experiments.
paper_title: Breaking Bottlenecks in Solid Electrolyte Discovery with Large Artificial Intelligence Models
